description:
Your role is that of an artifact-repository engineer who needs to isolate
  problem download requests and create a compact report for management.

  Resources already on disk
  -------------------------
  1.  A raw HTTP access log is located at
      /home/user/logs/artifact_audit.log
      Its lines follow this exact pattern  
         YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ SP repo-NAME/PATH/TO/FILE SP STATUS SP Human-text  
      • `repo-NAME` never contains slashes.  
      • `PATH/TO/FILE` can contain any byte except space.  
      • `STATUS` is the 3-digit HTTP status code.  
      Example line (already present in the file):
         2024-07-20T10:15:43Z repo-core/org/example/libbar-2.0.0.jar 404 Not Found

  Required output
  ---------------
  A. Create a new directory  
        /home/user/reports  
     and inside it create the **exactly‐named** file  
        /home/user/reports/bad_requests.log  

     bad_requests.log must contain **only** the requests whose status is
     either 404 or 500, in **the same order** they originally appeared.
     Each line of bad_requests.log must be rewritten into this
     four-column, tab-separated layout (no double spaces, use TABs only):

        TIMESTAMP<TAB>REPO_NAME<TAB>STATUS<TAB>RELATIVE_PATH

     where  
       • TIMESTAMP  ……  the timestamp copied unchanged from the source line  
       • REPO_NAME  ……  the text between the first space and the first slash  
       • STATUS     ……  the numeric status (404 or 500)  
       • RELATIVE_PATH … everything after “REPO_NAME/” up to the status code,
                          i.e. the artifact’s path inside the repo.  
     A newline must follow the final record.

     Example of the desired format (actual data will match the log):
        2024-07-20T10:15:43Z<TAB>repo-core<TAB>404<TAB>org/example/libbar-2.0.0.jar

  B. Also inside /home/user/reports create  
        /home/user/reports/bad_request_summary.txt  
     Each line of this summary must aggregate how many 404 and 500 events
     occurred per repository, sorted alphabetically by repository name.
     The exact required line format is:

        REPO_NAME 404:<count> 500:<count>

     Again, terminate the file with a trailing newline.

  C. While the script/commands are running, feel free to print intermediary
     console output, but the two files above will be the only artefacts
     verified by the automated tests.

  Technical constraints & hints
  -----------------------------
  • Solve everything with standard GNU/Linux user-land tools available in a
    minimal container (grep, sed, awk, sort, mkdir, etc.), no need for
    Python.  
  • Because both extraction (status filtering) and transformation
    (splitting fields) are required, a single compound/pipe command or a
    short script is expected.  
  • Use extended regular expressions (ERE) where useful; *do not* hard-code
    the example numbers because hidden tests may add more lines.  
  • Do *not* modify the original artifact_audit.log.  
  • Ensure that /home/user/reports/bad_requests.log and
    /home/user/reports/bad_request_summary.txt are readable by a regular
    user (default umask is fine).  

  Deliverables
  ------------
  1. /home/user/reports/bad_requests.log
  2. /home/user/reports/bad_request_summary.txt

  The automated grader will check:
  • that /home/user/reports exists,  
  • that both files exist,  
  • that bad_requests.log matches the required 4-column, tab-separated
    layout and contains only 404/500 lines,  
  • that bad_request_summary.txt has correct per-repo counts and is sorted
    alphabetically.

  Take your time—the task is complete once both files contain the right
  data in the right format.
truth:
Initial files present before the agent starts
  --------------------------------------------
  1. /home/user/logs/artifact_audit.log (permissions 0644):
     -----8<----- (exact content) -----
     2024-07-20T10:15:42Z repo-core/org/example/libfoo-1.2.3.jar 200 -
     2024-07-20T10:15:43Z repo-core/org/example/libbar-2.0.0.jar 404 Not Found
     2024-07-20T10:16:01Z repo-proprietary/com/acme/widget-5.1.0.bin 500 Internal Server Error
     2024-07-20T10:16:05Z repo-proprietary/com/acme/widget-5.1.0.sig 200 -
     2024-07-20T10:17:22Z repo-dev/io/experiment/test-0.0.1-alpha.jar 404 Not Found
     2024-07-20T10:18:55Z repo-core/org/example/libbaz-3.1.4.jar 200 -
     2024-07-20T10:19:04Z repo-core/org/example/libbar-2.0.0-sources.jar 404 Not Found
     -----8<----------------------------------------------

  Expected artefacts after successful completion
  ----------------------------------------------
  1. /home/user/reports/bad_requests.log (permissions 0644):
     -----8<----- (exact content, note TABs) -----
     2024-07-20T10:15:43Z	repo-core	404	org/example/libbar-2.0.0.jar
     2024-07-20T10:16:01Z	repo-proprietary	500	com/acme/widget-5.1.0.bin
     2024-07-20T10:17:22Z	repo-dev	404	io/experiment/test-0.0.1-alpha.jar
     2024-07-20T10:19:04Z	repo-core	404	org/example/libbar-2.0.0-sources.jar
     -----8<----------------------------------------------

  2. /home/user/reports/bad_request_summary.txt (permissions 0644):
     -----8<----- (exact content) -----
     repo-core 404:2 500:0
     repo-dev 404:1 500:0
     repo-proprietary 404:0 500:1
     -----8<----------------------------------------------

  The grader will diff these files byte-for-byte, allowing any POSIX newline
  (LF) at line ends but no carriage returns.  The presence of /home/user/reports
  itself is also mandatory.
